claims.text: GDACS's forest fire event report for Portugal (event 1029260, covering 02-08 July 2026) assessed an Orange alert, stating 'forest fire can have a medium humanitarian impact based on the burned area and the affected population and their vulnerability,' with 19,670 hectares burned and 9,361 people exposed in the burned area.

Re-checked 9 July 2026: GDACS's event report still shows a start date to last-detection window of 02 Jul 2026 to 08 Jul 2026 (duration 6 days), with 19,670 hectares burned and 9,361 people affected in the burned area unchanged; no thermal anomaly has been detected since 08 Jul 2026, indicating the fires are no longer actively spreading.

description: Multiple wildfires broke out across inland northern and central Portugal in early July 2026 amid a severe heatwave, drought-dry vegetation and strong winds, with the most serious fronts concentrated around the Viseu district. GDACS assessed an Orange alert for medium humanitarian impact as the fires spread. No further thermal anomalies have been detected since 8 July, indicating the fires are now contained.

impact.summary: GDACS's event report assessed an Orange alert (medium humanitarian impact) for wildfires burning across Portugal between 2 and 8 July 2026, citing about 19,670 hectares burned and roughly 9,361 people exposed within the fire-affected area. As of 9 July 2026, GDACS records no thermal anomaly detections since 8 July, indicating the fires are now contained.

location.region: Inland northern and central Portugal, centred near Viseu district

name: Wildfires — Portugal
